target.name: Distributed Spectrum
target.founder: Alex Wulff, Ben Harpe, Isaac Struhl

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Alex Wulff, Ben Harpe, and Isaac Struhl, having raised $25M to date. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 19 peer companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems. However, the peer universe lacks sufficient same-stage or comparable-tier comparables to support a reliable median-based approach.

Consequently, the valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total raised ($25M) as a value proxy and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $62M. This single method result anchors the analysis given the absence of viable peer multiples.

The triangulated range is $53M–$72M, centered on $62M. Key uncertainties include (1) the applicability of a 2.5x step-up multiplier given Distributed Spectrum's specific risk profile and market conditions; (2) limited visibility into Defense & Security peer capital structures and exit multiples at the seed stage; and (3) valuation volatility pending the next financing round. The analysis would benefit from additional seed-stage comparable data or clarification of near-term funding runway and milestones.
